projectDescription: A Cursor/agent skill that answers 'how does X work?' questions by spawning parallel explorer agents, synthesizing their findings into structured architectural explanations, and optionally running multi-model critique passes.
keyFeatures: Automatic complexity detection — simple questions run end-to-end in a single agent; complex ones fan out to 2–4 parallel explorer agents
Explain mode — produces structured architectural explanations with Overview, Key Concepts, How It Works, Where Things Live, and Gotchas sections
Critique mode — after explaining, spawns independent critics across multiple AI models (claude-opus-4.6, composer-2, gpt-5.4) to surface architectural issues
Lead judgment synthesis — categorizes critic findings into Act On, Consider, Noted, and Dismissed verdicts
Works with Cursor's plugin system via .cursor-plugin/plugin.json and also supports generic agent runners via .agents/
Prompt templates in references/ folder for explorer, explainer, critic subagents, and the architectural critique rubric
publicApiSurface: SKILL.md — top-level routing logic (explain vs. critique, simple vs. complex)
references/explorer-prompt.md — prompt template for parallel explorer subagents
references/explainer-prompt.md — prompt template for synthesis/explainer subagent
references/critic-prompt.md — prompt template for architectural critic subagents
references/critique-rubric.md — rubric covering Abstraction Fit, Data Model, Boundary Discipline, Evolution Readiness, Complexity vs. Value, Consistency
